So I want to teach you that strategy today It works equally well in person and over the phone and it relies on the fact that the Conscious mind, which is you know what you're focusing on right now your conscious mind Which is limited in processing power, right? When you are focusing on what someone is saying That's your conscious mind, which is listening actively interpreting those words trying to understand the meeting You're hanging on the prospects every word and your conscious mind has relatively limited processing power okay, in other words, it's very easily maxed out and The equivalent would be when you know, you're on a computer that has you know, RAM random access memory Which are the tests that you're doing right now Well, you know that spinning wheel of death you get in a computer Well, when you max out a computer's short-term memory, it's RAM The thing freezes up when the wheel spins and nothing happens, right? You've overloaded the sensory database basically it can't take it any more information So it ceases to operate right well on many levels when you are operating with an individual, right? Because the human brain's an operating system, right? What happens when it maxes out is not a spinning wheel of death, but all information Bypasses the conscious mind and goes straight to the unconscious mind which has infinite Processing power and this is once you understand this fact It's interplay between the conscious mind and the unconscious unconscious mind when it comes to communication Your closing it will fly up because what you need to realize is that you're always speaking to both minds and What happens is is that most people are typically speaking to only one line when you speak to both the conscious and the unconscious But yeah, and you have congruency You can move someone both emotionally and logically you could do it separately You could do it together, but the impact it has on your closing rate is absolutely staggering Let me give you an example here. I'm not thinking about any of that stuff my entire Physiology is being run by my unconscious mind which has infinite processing power as Doing all that stuff. You my brain is working over time Constantly standing vigil even when I'm asleep it makes sure I breathe the right way. Okay, make sure I don't pee in my bed It holds it does everything for me Including and here's the part where it gets interesting Including the fact that while I yes, I can only take in a little bit of this picture the rest of the picture Goes to my mind anyway See my eyes my ears my five senses take in all the data But there's simply too much data for the conscious mind to take in so the rest of the bypass might my conscious mind essentially deletes out the rest and Then it flows directly to my unconscious mind where it generalizes things and puts them into patterns It takes everything around you and goes into the deepest recesses. You start to form patterns These patterns are what is crucial to those first four seconds in other words we have developed patterns of behavior and Patterns of how we move through the world for everything for instance when I walk up to a door that I've never seen before a strange door I don't say whoa What is this thing? But the point is is that it starts in these first few seconds And even on in the system here like you know the the monthly Trainings right we have all the archives. I go through this stuff in great detail, right? So you can search for it as well, right? The point is this it starts with the first four seconds But what happens after that? What's really happening the second part of it? Even though 10 minutes or so before I start Q&A here and I want to go through what is really happening In a sale, okay? So we essentially I keep saying to you that this opens up the possibility of influence, right? Well, what does that really mean? Well essentially The biggest battle I think for most salespeople is they really don't know what what selling is about They don't understand what's going on when they sell so they kind of scratch their head Looking for a strategy, right and strategy. Listen strategy strategy. It works. You can you can model strategies. You can duplicate strategies But my experience is that unless you understand the reason behind the strategy It's very difficult to apply the strategy at the highest level even in a reasonably effective way It's almost like, you know, you don't know why you're doing what you're doing and it sort of takes away the context of it all It makes it less effective. So I want you to really understand what's happening when you're selling and the best Way to explain it is that essentially what's happening is like There's this law of physics called the law of thermodynamics and it's about the transfer of energy the transfer of heat And they always say that heat flows from hot to cold Not the other way around Something is hot That energy that excess energy the the movement it flows over and heats up something. It's cold Cold doesn't overtake hot heat Will flow to cold right one of the laws of nature of thermodynamics, right? What is happening? Imagine a sales account, right blank slate, right? And right here this is your open where the sale begins And this is your close where the profit says. Yeah, let's do it, right? So this is your open That's your close, right? And I always put these little arrows here. You give me a rebel, please So I'm gonna be rebel. Thanks. Um, I always have these arrows, right? And these arrows represent What I basically say is the perfect sale What basically the client is, you know, almost pre-sold at everything you say Everything you do the prospects keeps saying yes. Yes. Yes. They're like a yes machine, right? No objections all the way to the close and that's we refer to As thanks buddy as a perfect straight line sale and take a sip of this real quick. Here's some energy This is what we refer to as the perfect straight line sale We basically everything you say the client says, yes. Oh, wow, sounds great. Oh my god. What a good. Oh, wow. Oh, that sounds interesting. Oh my god Oh, what a beautiful product. Oh my god. Wow great. And they say, yeah, here's my credit card and they buy No objections. That's the perfect straight line sale But that those sales are far and few between What typically happens is your prospect tries to take you off the straight line. They have questions They have objections. They have concerns. They interrupt you You want to keep them on the straight line? They try to take you off the straight line So what we have here are these healthy boundaries above and below the line When you're inside these boundaries here and here your in this is being called in control Of the sale you're in control. This is what influence occurs when you're inside these values When you cross outside these boundaries, you go off the right called pluto up here Or down here to your anus not a good place to be for most of us, right? Although I don't judge you want to go to your anus. God bless you. Okay So what this basically means is that these are the healthy places of influence, right? And what's happening here When you move someone down the straight line from the open to the close, essentially what you're doing is you're transferring the emotion of certainty You're transferring the certainty that you feel so let's say, you know, you let's say you're selling a product xyz product, right? Well, when you show up to this thing, so imagine this is the encounter, right? And the prospect enters here and you enter here But what level of certainty is the prospect at? The answer who knows I mean, we don't know like let's say you're selling life insurance, right? And you call up someone and you try to sell them life insurance Where how certain are they about life insurance and your product? The answer is you don't know What you go there somewhere. I mean imagine a one represents uncertainty absolute uncertainty a 10 represents a state of absolute So they're absolutely sure that you'll progress the best thing in the world here And over on this side of the equation, they're absolutely certain that your product is the biggest piece of crap ever Right, they hate you. They hate your product. They love your product, right? Now, obviously When you ask for the order you'd like to have as close to as possible as a to attend right now You're chance of closing it infinitely better, right? But you don't know where they are when they enter are they at a six or seven or five full? Who really knows see that? Number where they enter is based on what's called their preconceived notions They have preconceived notions based on what they've seen heard all the things, you know in the unconscious mind all the things they've ever seen they as soon as they hear about your product they bring instantly sizes of all past experiences and Lends them somewhere on the certain scale. Maybe it's a four. Maybe it's a six. Maybe it's a seven Depending on what they know about your product depending on what they know about The industry about the specifics of your product or products like that in general It could be about the sales person themselves. They trust sales will they have a certain level of certainty They bring into this encounter The question then is what level of certainty are you at? Will you enter the encounter at absolute 10? You want to be absolutely certain? And damn sure of it. That's where you are So they enter at this very low level of certainty You enter at a very high level of certainty The highest possible right and what you're doing is every word you say Everything you do is designed to transfer this certainty To your prospect so that when you get to the end and ask for the order at least you know at one two or three times you can ask me You're Moving them from a four to a ten. You're looking to increase their level of so you're transferring The certainty inside of you the best metaphor for this All right, and then we'll start to Q and A. It's going to give you a nice metaphor here Nice metaphor here. Okay is the way a home Heating system works. There's let's say you have a house, right? And the house has got a boiler, right and the boiler has basically All is like this really great, you know, like um, you know, it's like state of the art, right? It creates heat whether it uses oil or whatever it uses, right? Fucking never breaks, right? And then you have all this piping The hvac that runs throughout the home So the boiler which creates the heat or the furnace is in the basement, right? Then you have pipes and duct work and what happens is the pipes and the duct work is what allows the heat to travel from the furnace To all the various rooms of the house So the heat doesn't get dissipated lonely imagine if there was no duct work Well, what happened you just have a furnace heating up the air around it and it would be inefficient It would be very difficult to transfer heat from a furnace to each individual room Without having the duct work, right? So that's how things work. But what happens is this let's say the furnace is broken one day Can you heat the house? No You have this incredible duct work. That's all laid out as infrastructure, right? But if there's no heat coming from the furnace, you can't heat the house the house stays cold The same is true of sales Imagine the straight line system, right? It's this powerful perfect way to transfer certainty from you To any other human being that's it could transfer it transfers certainty But where does the certainty come from? Does it come from the straight line system? No The straight line system doesn't create certainty The straight line system transfers certainty Doesn't create a transfers certainty So what happens is you have to enter the encounter In a state of absolute certainty about your product looking certain feeling certain talking certain you're in a state of absolute certainty you're at a 10 on that certain skill and then what happens is As you move them down the straight line, you are transferring the certainty to them Raising them up on the certainty scale. So they approach a 10 At which point you have a shot to close them That's a simplified version. It's a very powerful metaphor. In other words, what's happening as you move someone down the straight line You're transferring emotion That's it sales. My friend is the transference of emotion And the primary emotion that you transfer Is certainty There's certainty about your product that it's the best fit for them Best price best cost benefit ratio best value proposition certainty about you the sales person that you're a person they want to deal with that you'll be There for the long term you're ethical. You're honest. You're good for service. So so certainty for the product certainty for you Certainty for the company that stands behind the product Right. It's got to be a company behind the product What you're looking to do as you move someone down the straight line From the open to the close You're transferring certainty Using the straight line the straight line doesn't create certainty. It transfers certainty You have to show up in a state of absolute certainty and start in those first four seconds By sounding sharp by being on the bull by sounding enthusiastic by coming off as an expert in your field that opens up the possibility If you then to transfer this certainty be perceived as an expert and bam the rest as they say is history At the highest level comes down that straight line system Is your hand bones that weigh in you come back tomorrow. The basic elements are is is is essentially each time you enter in the next phase You have to sort of button up certainty again. You keep the move, you know, it's almost like you're backing and filling So when you have your first call you're gathering intelligence You're framing your antilogical case you're sending any information and you're meeting decision makers next call You have to start off. You don't just start off where you left off You back up and reaffirm the intelligence you gather and then you go forward again Next time you do it you go forward again Now that's one aspect also your multiple decision makers that are going through that's sometimes what the case is There's three or four people So you need to be getting in touch with all decision makers at once Then there's also little touch points along the way that you know, not every call is going to be a sales call Someone's just calling with old schmooze calls But again every sales a bit different in the sense that the same things have to line up But the cycle and what's driving it is different and based on that you'll run your straight line pattern Slightly slightly differently and also need to make sure that who are the multiple influences Who are the people that are making this decision most long sales cycles are because of multiple decision makers You want to know who those decision makers are and and getting to each one independently and making a pitch to them All right All right, this one's from the maxwell team. I love it How long should the first sales presentation be and how long should the subsequent loops be generally speaking? So Again, it's not a one size fits all proposition, right? You know, they're on the certification among you're actually writing the scripts with them, which is awesome Um So a rule of thumb Can I give you a stupid answer the stupid answer is it shouldn't be any longer that it has to be in no shorter than it should be Because some of this is logic by the way. In other words, remember this right? You're framing an airtight logical case now. I know a little bit about your business Right, I'm going to know a lot more about it when I meet with you guys intimately right in person, right? My guess is that the initial presentation Should not be longer than seven minutes The initial Present at the at the longest, okay? And then you'll be asking for your order at the seven minute mark, okay? And then the loops start from there and each loop is probably around five to ten minutes long depending The first loop is probably the longest one is like six or seven minutes And then then they get shorter thereafter But a sale should take no more than 20 minutes You know a tough sale should take no more than 20 to 25 minutes.
